meaning: adj.正确的,恰当的,端正的vt.改正,纠正,告诫,惩戒
examples: Only one of the answers is correct.
I think you've made the correct decision.
If you spot any spurious information, demand that the credit bureau investigate and correct it.
If Cao is correct and is able to execute his idea, Internet addresses could be toast by 2010.
